mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above the 5.0 percent threshold signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer spending power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, which drives positive equity returns through higher revenue growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month signals yield negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading above its twelve-month moving average signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, supporting positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during supply-constrained or inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real interest rates calculated from the federal funds rate minus annualized CPI growth signal monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive real rates coincide with positive SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of observations over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects stable economic activity and wage expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with twenty-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding two point five percent indicates strong aggregate demand, which translates into higher earnings forecasts and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: High GDP growth periods fail to produce positive sixty-day returns in greater than fifty percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals productive capacity expansion without labor market overheating, creating favorable conditions for equity appreciation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rising MoM and UNRATE falling MoM
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative forward returns in greater than fifty-five percent of triggered instances.
